title: Phaenopyrum parvifolium
intro: Phaenopyrum parvifolium, also known as Chinese Lovage or Small-Leaf Phaenopyrum, is a plant species native to China and parts of Asia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to promote health and wellness, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Phaenopyrum parvifolium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Phaenopyrum parvifolium is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into various herbal formulas to support overall health and well-being.

Additionally, Phaenopyrum parvifolium can also be classified as a member of the tribe Paniceae.
